name: Kahn's Topological Sort (BFS)
summary: Take every course with no remaining prerequisites; each one taken clears its dependents. If all courses get taken, there's no cycle.
visualizationIntent: init: draw a node per course and a directed edge b→a for each prerequisite; compute each course's in-degree; queue every course with in-degree 0 (frontier). loop: take the front course (current, then visited) and, for each course that depended on it, drop that course's in-degree. update: a course reaching in-degree 0 becomes ready and joins the queue. return: true if every course was taken, false if a cycle left some course permanently blocked.

lineExplanations.3: Start an empty list of course nodes for the graph.
lineExplanations.5: Add a node for this course, labeled with its number.
lineExplanations.6: Start an in-degree list — how many prerequisites each course still has.
lineExplanations.7: Loop over every course to give it an in-degree slot.
lineExplanations.9: Start an empty list of directed edges.
lineExplanations.11: a is the course that has the prerequisite.
lineExplanations.12: b is the course that must come first.
lineExplanations.13: Add a directed edge b → a (finish b before a).
lineExplanations.14: Course a now has one more prerequisite to clear.
lineExplanations.15: Start a status list (0 = blocked, 1 = ready/queued, 2 = taken).
lineExplanations.16: Loop over every course to give it a status slot.
lineExplanations.18: Create the queue of courses ready to take.
lineExplanations.19: head marks the front of the queue.
lineExplanations.20: Loop over every course to find the ones with no prerequisites.
lineExplanations.21: Does this course have in-degree 0 (nothing blocking it)?
lineExplanations.22: Queue it — it can be taken right away.
lineExplanations.24: Placeholder for the course being taken; -1 means 'none'.
lineExplanations.25: Count how many courses have been taken so far.
lineExplanations.26: Keep taking courses while the ready-queue isn't empty.
lineExplanations.27: Look at the course at the front of the queue.
lineExplanations.29: Remember it as the course being taken now.
lineExplanations.32: Scan every edge to find courses that depended on this one.
lineExplanations.33: Does this edge start at the course we just took?
lineExplanations.34: nb is the dependent course at the edge's far end.
lineExplanations.35: Clear one prerequisite from that dependent course.
lineExplanations.36: Did clearing it drop the dependent to in-degree 0?
lineExplanations.37: It's now ready — add it to the queue.
lineExplanations.39: Done taking this course — hide the cursor.
lineExplanations.40: We can finish only if every course was taken.
lineExplanations.41: Return whether all courses were completed.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: is the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def canFinish(self, numCourses, prerequisites): numCourses is the count, prerequisites is a list of [a,b] pairs.
syntaxExplanations.3: [] creates the list of node objects.
syntaxExplanations.4: for i in range(numCourses): loops i over each course id.
syntaxExplanations.5: A dict literal {"id": i, "label": i} describes one graph node.
syntaxExplanations.7: for i in range(numCourses): loops over the courses.
syntaxExplanations.8: indegree.append(0) starts each course at zero prerequisites.
syntaxExplanations.10: for p in range(len(prerequisites)): loops over each pair.
syntaxExplanations.11: prerequisites[p][0] reads the first number of the pair (a).
syntaxExplanations.13: A dict {"from": b, "to": a} is a directed edge b → a.
syntaxExplanations.14: indegree[a] = indegree[a] + 1 records a's extra prerequisite.
syntaxExplanations.16: for i in range(numCourses): loops over the courses.
syntaxExplanations.17: status.append(0) marks each course blocked to start.
syntaxExplanations.19: head = 0 starts the queue's front pointer.
syntaxExplanations.20: for i in range(numCourses): loops over the courses.
syntaxExplanations.21: if indegree[i] == 0: tests for no remaining prerequisites.
syntaxExplanations.24: cur = -1 sets a sentinel; -1 is not a valid course id.
syntaxExplanations.26: while head < len(queue): runs while ready courses remain.
syntaxExplanations.27: queue[head] reads the front course id.
syntaxExplanations.29: cur = node_id remembers the course being taken.
syntaxExplanations.32: for e in range(len(edges)): scans every edge.
syntaxExplanations.33: edges[e]["from"] == node_id finds edges leaving this course.
syntaxExplanations.34: edges[e]["to"] is the dependent course id.
syntaxExplanations.35: indegree[nb] = indegree[nb] - 1 clears one of its prerequisites.
syntaxExplanations.36: if indegree[nb] == 0: checks whether it's now unblocked.
syntaxExplanations.37: queue.append(nb) makes the freed course ready.
syntaxExplanations.39: cur = -1 hides the cursor between courses.
syntaxExplanations.40: taken == numCourses is True only if every course was taken.
syntaxExplanations.41: return result hands back the answer.
